sections.title: Complete Mantra
sections.sanskrit: ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचodayात्॥
sections.transliteration: Om bhur bhuvah swah tat-savitur varenyam bhargo devasya dheemahi dhiyo yo nah prachodayat
sections.meaning: Detailed explanation of the mantra's meaning, word by word translation, and overall spiritual significance.

sections.title: Origin and History
sections.content: Detailed history of the mantra, which scripture it comes from, who revealed it, and its ancient significance.

sections.title: How to Chant This Mantra
sections.content: Complete guidelines for proper chanting including pronunciation, posture, and mindset.
steps: Purify yourself with a bath or washing hands/feet
Sit in a comfortable meditative posture facing east or north
Take a few deep breaths to calm the mind
Begin with Om chanting to set the vibration
Chant the mantra slowly with full attention on each syllable
Use a rudraksha or tulsi mala for counting (108 times)
After completion, sit in silence for a few minutes
sections.bestTime: Brahma Muhurta (4-6 AM) or during Sandhya (sunrise/sunset)

items.description: Awakens spiritual energy and connects with divine consciousness
Improves concentration, memory, and mental clarity
Regulates breathing and reduces stress hormones
Removes obstacles and brings success in endeavors

sections.title: Scientific Research
sections.content: Modern scientific studies on the effects of this mantra on brain waves, stress levels, and overall wellness.
